title: Sphaerostephanos bulusanicus
intro: Sphaerostephanos bulusanicus, also known as Bulusan fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Sphaerostephanos bulusanicus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sphaerostephanos bulusanicus is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat fever, skin infections, and other health conditions.
taxonomy: Sphaerostephanos bulusanicus is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Sphaerostephanos, which is a group of ferns known for their unique and diverse species. Sphaerostephanos bulusanicus is a member of the class Polypodiopsida, which includes all ferns and their allies. The plant's classification is further divided into the subclass Polypodiidae, the order Polypodiales, and the subclass Polypodiidae. By understanding the classification of Sphaerostephanos bulusanicus, we can gain insights into its evolutionary history and relationships with other fern species.
